qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, admitted for kidney stone, medication for about a week.

critical_issues: MER discrepancy for kidney stone recovery date: Customer states 2 years ago (2024), but MER states "More than 2 years ago".
MER discrepancy for eye condition: Customer denies eye diseases, but MER records "Hyperopia" as a 'Yes' for the disease list, likely an inference from spectacles mentioned later.
MER discrepancy for medication/treatment lasting more than 2 weeks: Customer clearly disclosed hospital admission and medication for kidney stone, but MER recorded "No".

MER discrepancy for the open-ended question "Is there anything else...": Customer provided general health/habit information, but MER specifically captured "Annual Health Checkup", which was then asked as a separate question.
recommendations: Ensure MER accurately reflects all customer disclosures, especially for medical history and treatments.

Clarify the mapping of general health questions (e.g., "any eye defect") to specific conditions (e.g., "Hyperopia") in the MER, or add specific questions for vision correction (e.g., "Do you wear spectacles/contact lenses?").
Review the process for capturing open-ended "anything else" questions to ensure all relevant details are documented.
Ensure the doctor's questions align precisely with the MER structure to avoid misinterpretations or incomplete data capture.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and provides detailed answers.
Customer expresses gratitude at the end of the call.

validation_errors: Kidney stone recovery date: Customer stated '2024' (2 years ago from call date 2026), but MER recorded 'More than 2 years ago'.
